case_title: MADELIENE L MITCHELL VS. UAL CORP/UNITED AIRLINES

docket_entries.description: MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-26-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT U.A.L. CORP./ UNITED AIRLINES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH THIRTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST SEPARATE THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT FROM THE TORT CAUSES OF ACTION. WITH RESPECT TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST CLARIFY WHAT TERMS WERE BREACHED AND WHETHER THE TERMS WERE WRITTEN, ORAL, IMPLIED OR SOME COMBINATION THEREOF. TO PLEAD A FORM OF EMPLOYMENT DISCRIMINATION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD THAT SHE WAS PERFORMING COMPETENTLY AND THAT SHE SUFFERED ADVERSE ACTION THAT WAS CAUSALLY RELATED TO HER PROTECTED STATUS. THE UNRUH CIVIL RIGHTS ACT DOES NOT APPLY TO EMPLOYMENT CASES AND PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS BASED ON THAT ACT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: NICHOLE RODICH, CSR 11604
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT UAL CORP/ UNITED AIRLINES, INC. ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 7.0 DAYS
